new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine with a protected designation of origin (PDO), produced from grapes of the 'Glera' vine variety harvested in the regions of the designations of origin 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', and 'Asolo — Prosecco'; classified under CN code 2204 10 15 (Chapter 22).

Quality representing the protected geographic origin of Prosecco, covering the designated regions 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', and 'Asolo — Prosecco' in Italy, as required for PDO classification of Prosecco sparkling wine under CN 2204 10 15.
Quality representing the use of grapes exclusively from the 'Glera' vine variety, as required for the production of Prosecco PDO sparkling wine classified under CN 2204 10 15.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture all key distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) Prosecco is a subclass of SparklingWine (parent hierarchy), (2) it has a protected designation of origin (PDO) quality linked to the four specific Italian regions via ProseccoRegionOrigin, (3) it is produced exclusively from the 'Glera' vine variety captured via GleraGrapeVariety, and (4) its CN code 22041015 is asserted via hasValue. The coverage is set to 0.95 rather than 1.0 because the PDO status itself (as a formal regulatory designation status, distinct from mere geographic origin) is not fully reified as a separate quality class — it is approximated through the geographic origin quality. The four specific region names are named in the definition but not individually modelled as named individuals, which is an acceptable trade-off for TBox-level modelling.
